authoritative_sources.title: Business Guide to the FTC's Mail, Internet, or Telephone Order Merchandise Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator for remote-order shipment promises, delay notices, cancellations, and prompt refunds that directly affect electronics order-status and delayed-delivery support responses.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: order-status clarification, delay handling, and refund-status boundary setting

authoritative_sources.title: Businessperson's Guide to Federal Warranty Law
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC guidance is the federal baseline for Magnuson-Moss warranty obligations and seller-versus-warrantor boundaries that shape what customer support may safely say about exchanges, repairs, and warranty outcomes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: warranty-status communication, replacement-versus-repair boundary handling, and seller-role clarification

authoritative_sources.title: California Civil Code Section 1723
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official California statutory text is authoritative for posted refund-policy rules and fallback remedies in a major retail jurisdiction, which constrains support responses when posted policy and state law may diverge.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: refund-policy conflict review and jurisdiction-sensitive complaint handling

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. consumer-finance regulator for billing-error guidance and is authoritative for dispute timelines and merchant-versus-issuer boundaries relevant to refund-not-posted complaints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment mismatch, refund-status handling, and card-billing escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Recall Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC is the primary U.S. federal safety regulator for many consumer products and its recall handbook is the official public guide for recall remedies, stop-use framing, and consumer-facing recall communication.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: recall-related customer support, stop-use boundaries, and safety escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Reverse Logistics Brochure
authoritative_sources.publisher: Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PHMSA is the primary federal authority for hazardous-material transportation, so its reverse-logistics guidance is authoritative when damaged or lithium-battery products affect return or support instructions.

authoritative_sources.title: Fighting Identity Theft with the Red Flags Rule: A How-To Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC Red Flags guidance is an authoritative public control reference for suspicious identity and account-use signals that affect refund rerouting, address changes, and other support actions with fraud exposure.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: identity-mismatch handling and fraud-sensitive support escalation

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm channel, jurisdiction, customer context, order or service identifiers, and whether the request is in scope, blocked, or owned by another lane before responding.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Order, payment, promotion, and warranty record review
workflow_stages.description: Validate receipts, order state, tender history, refund status, promotion impacts, warranty posture, and service-ticket status so the support answer stays tied to authoritative records.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Supported-resolution and communication packaging
workflow_stages.description: Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes and customer communication summaries that describe what is known, what was done, and what the customer must do next without overstating finality.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Exception, complaint, and repeat-failure escalation
workflow_stages.description: Move unsupported refund asks, service-recovery demands, repeat failures, and policy conflicts into explicit escalation packages with the correct owner and evidence set.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Safety, fraud, and loss-prevention stop-condition handling
workflow_stages.description: Stop unsupported actions and escalate whenever recall, damaged-battery, suspicious identity, refund reroute, or loss-prevention indicators change the safe answer.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Handoff, closure, and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Confirm what counts as resolved versus handed off, record the next owner, and preserve public-baseline constraints when tenant-specific service programs or policies are applied.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: orders, receipts, invoices, customer profiles, and contact history
delivery windows, installation bookings, service tickets, repair-status notes, and appointment records
payment-tender history, refund status, gift-card or financing references, and promotion records
serial number, IMEI, model, SKU, warranty, protection-plan, and RMA artifacts
inventory availability, substitute options, backorder notices, and service recovery notes
recall notices, damaged-device flags, fraud or loss-prevention notes, and escalation records

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, complaint-driven, exception-driven, or owner-transfer sensitive.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Provide evidence-backed status, policy-aware next-step summaries, and customer communication without overstating authority.
Prepare escalation packages and handoff summaries when ownership shifts.
escalate: Required order, payment, warranty, promotion, service-ticket, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires pricing override, refund exception approval, service-recovery approval, legal interpretation, fraud adjudication, or safety signoff.
A recall, damaged-battery, suspicious identity, or loss-prevention signal changes whether customer support can act directly.
Public baseline guidance and tenant, manufacturer, lender, or jurisdiction policy materially conflict.
refuse: Promise a refund, replacement, or compensation outcome that the authoritative record does not support.
Override pricing, refund, promotion, safety, or fraud controls without explicit authority.
Authorize continued use, shipment, or handling of recalled or damaged products.

domain_failure_modes: the lane promises a refund or replacement when POS, OMS, or policy records do not support it
a payment mismatch is handled as routine customer service instead of being routed into the right billing-dispute path
a support case closes without the minimum order, ticket, or warranty evidence
repeat service failures are treated as ordinary status checks instead of escalation-worthy complaints
support gives unsafe reassurance on recalled or damaged products
fraud or identity-mismatch indicators are ignored in the name of convenience
the industry label is cosmetic and the pack collapses into generic customer support instead of electronics and appliance terminology and evidence handling

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State refund-law differences, manufacturer remedy programs, tenant premium-service programs, and lender or card-network rules can materially narrow the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack uses FTC, CFPB, California, CPSC, PHMSA, and FTC Red Flags guidance as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve tenant jurisdiction, manufacturer program, financing context, and service-policy precedence before applying a tenant-specific outcome.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure behavior instead of only generic customer-service scripting.
The lane stops at support resolution and communication. Refund exception approval, discretionary service recovery, fraud findings, repair diagnosis, and safety authority remain outside the lane.
